Monsignor Laurent Ulrich, Archbishop of Paris, ordained nine new priests.Jun 27, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.notredamedeparis.fr/en/priestly-ordinations-at-notre-dame-de-paris/#:~:text=On%20Saturday%2027%20June%202026,Paris%2C%20ordained%20nine%20new%20priests.","title":"Priestly Ordinations at Notre-Dame de Paris - Notre-Dame de Paris","content":"On Saturday 27 June 2026 at 9.30 am, Notre-Dame de Paris hosted the priestly ordinations for the Diocese of Paris.
Monsignor Laurent Ulrich, Archbishop of Paris, ordained nine new priests.
Storage or technical access is strictly necessary for the legitimate interest of enabling the use of a specific service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user, or solely for the purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network."},{"url":"https://thecatholicherald.com/article/diocese-of-paris-marks-twenty-year-high-at-first-notre-dame-ordinations-since-fire","title":"Diocese of Paris marks twenty-year high at first Notre-Dame ordinations since fire","content":"On 28 June 2025, the eve of the feast of St Peter and St Paul, in Notre-Dame Cathedral, Paris, Archbishop Laurent Ulrich, the Archbishop of Paris, ordained 16 men to the Sacred Priesthood.
These were the first ordinations held in the historic cathedral since it was nearly destroyed by fire in 2019 and subsequently restored.
Around 5,000 people attended, with approximately 2,000 inside Notre-Dame and another 3,000 watching from the forecourt on large screens.
The 16 new priests, aged between 27 and 42, come from a variety of professional backgrounds.
Eight belong to religious communities, with half of those coming from the Emmanuel Community, a French Catholic association of the faithful of pontifical right, founded in 1972 by Pierre Goursat and Martine Lafitte-Catta.
They represent a significant increase in ordinations compared with 2024, when only six men were ordained for the diocese.
Among religious communities, the Community of Saint Martin will see the most new priests, with nine men being ordained.
France’s Catholic Church welcomed more than 10,384 adult catechumens at Easter this year, marking a 45 per cent increase from 2024 and the highest number since records began."},{"url":"https://fsspx.news/en/news/notre-dame-paris-reopening-schedule-42366","title":"Notre-Dame de Paris: The Reopening Schedule | FSSPX News","content":"The reopening period will extend from December 8, 2024 to June 8, 2025, the feast of Pentecost, and will include numerous celebrations and pilgrimages, while the entire Church will experience a jubilee year.
Archbishop of Paris since 2022, Ulrich finally thanked the generous donors and patrons, large and small, who contributed to the reconstruction of the stone cathedral."},{"url":"https://www.vaticannews.va/en/pope/news/2022-04/pope-names-laurent-ulrich-as-new-archbishop-of-paris.html","title":"Pope names Laurent Ulrich as new Archbishop of Paris - Vatican News","content":"Apr 26, 2022 — In 2019, the Archdiocese of Paris had approximately 100 parishes, 492 incardinated priests, 126 permanent deacons, 67 seminarians in formation, ...Read more
On 6 June 2000, John Paul II appointed Father Ulrich Archbishop of Chambéry, and Bishop of Saint-Jean-de-Maurienne-Tarentaise.
His episcopal ordination took place on 10 September 2000.
Within the archdiocese, he served as President of Catholic Education and Chancellor of the Catholic University of Lille.
In July 2019, he became president of the Council for Catholic Education.
Archbishop Aupetit submitted his resignation from the pastoral governance of the archdiocese to Pope Francis in a letter on 25 November 2021, which the Pope accepted on 2 December.
In 2019, the Archdiocese of Paris had approximately 100 parishes, 492 incardinated priests, 126 permanent deacons, 67 seminarians in formation, 473 priests who are members of religious institutes, 179 men religious who are not priests, and 1,351 women religious."}]}
